title: 1. Numbers and algebra
text: ‘Numbers and algebra’ introduces the number system and explains several terms used in algebra, including natural numbers, positive and negative integers, rational numbers, number factorization, the Fundamental Theorem of Arithmetic, Euclid’s Lemma, the Division Algorithm, and the Euclidean Algorithm. It proves that any common factor c of a and b is also a factor of any number of the form ax + by, and since the greatest common divisor (gcd) of a and b has this form, which may be found by reversing the steps of the Euclidean Algorithm, it follows that any common factor c of a and b divides their gcd d.

title: Algebra
text: Algebra is a branch of mathematics that deals with abstract systems, known as algebraic structures, and the manipulation of expressions within those systems. It is a generalization of arithmetic that introduces variables and algebraic operations other than the standard arithmetic operations, such as addition and multiplication.
